licenses.name: Eclipse Public License 2.0
licenses.description: File-level weak copyleft; modifications to EPL files must be shared. Allows combining with GPL-2.0+ under certain conditions.

compatibility.notes: Can be combined with GPL-2.0+ via secondary license provision. File-level copyleft similar to MPL. Includes explicit patent grant and patent termination clause.

metadata.explanation_hint: Used by Eclipse IDE, Jakarta EE, and many Java projects. Includes an explicit patent grant and a patent termination clause. Copyleft is per 'module', not per file.

licenses.name: zlib License
licenses.description: Very permissive; modified versions must be marked as changed and cannot be misrepresented as original. Common in game development.

compatibility.notes: Highly permissive. Modified versions must be marked as changed but no copyleft or attribution required in binaries. Compatible with most licenses.

metadata.explanation_hint: Used by zlib, libpng, SDL. Unlike MIT, you must mark modified sources as changed. No attribution required in binaries — common in game engines and embedded systems.

licenses.name: PostgreSQL License
licenses.description: Short permissive license similar to MIT/BSD, used exclusively by the PostgreSQL project.

compatibility.notes: Functionally identical to MIT. Compatible with virtually all licenses. Used exclusively by PostgreSQL — no practical reason to use it for new projects.

metadata.explanation_hint: Essentially MIT-equivalent. Used only by PostgreSQL. No practical reason to use it for new projects — choose MIT or BSD-2 instead.

licenses.name: Artistic License 2.0
licenses.description: Permissive license with an optional copyleft clause for modified standard versions, used by Perl and Raku.

compatibility.notes: Used by Perl 5 and Raku. Allows distribution under modified terms but modified standard versions must be distributed under Artistic-2.0.

metadata.explanation_hint: Perl/Raku ecosystem license. Allows proprietary distribution but modified standard versions must stay Artistic-2.0. Consider MIT for simpler permissive terms.

licenses.name: Mozilla Public License 2.0
licenses.description: File-level copyleft: modified MPL files must remain open, but can be combined with proprietary code.

compatibility.notes: File-level copyleft. Modified MPL files must stay MPL, but can link with proprietary code. Compatible with GPL. Incompatible with permissive licenses due to copyleft.

metadata.explanation_hint: Used by Firefox, Thunderbird, and many Mozilla projects. Ideal for mixed open/proprietary codebases where you want file-level copyleft.

licenses.name: Common Development and Distribution License 1.0
licenses.description: File-level copyleft from Sun Microsystems; used for OpenSolaris and ZFS. Modifications to covered files must be shared.

compatibility.notes: INCOMPATIBLE with GPL (FSF says so explicitly). File-level copyleft similar to MPL. Historically significant for ZFS but a major source of controversy.

metadata.explanation_hint: Historically used by Sun/Oracle for ZFS and NetBeans. INCOMPATIBLE with GPL (FSF says so explicitly) -- major source of controversy when Linux wanted ZFS. Not recommended for new projects.
